description.p: 1984 is a cycle-stepped Amstrad CPC 464/6128 emulator written in C with SDL3. The core machine — Z80 CPU, MC6845 CRTC, AY-3-8912 PSG, uPD765 FDC, Gate Array, keyboard, joystick/gamepad and cassette tape — runs commercial games and standard software at full speed.

ul.li: DS12887 real-time clock (Cyboard / SYMBiFACE II compatible)
SYMBiFACE II / Cyboard IDE (raw disk images, FAT16/FAT32)
Albireo USB host (CH376) for UNIDOS and SymbOS

description.p: HDCPM / CP/M+ boots fully to the A0:CPM> prompt from FAT-formatted SYMBiFACE II / Cyboard IDE drives, mounting up to four CPMDSKxx.IMG images. SymbOS desktop runs on both Albireo (CH376 USB) and Cyboard (Net4CPC + RTC + SYMBiFACE IDE). Two CPU/interrupt fixes underneath: the Gate Array IRQ acknowledge is now deferred until the Z80 actually accepts the maskable interrupt (so the 300 Hz IRQ cadence stays stable across IFF1-masked sections), and RETI restores IFF1 from IFF2 like Caprice32 / WinAPE / MAME.
Cassette tape support (.cdt/TZX) with loading screech mixed into PSG audio; fullscreen letterbox gutter colour fix.
